req.title: Transfer funds between own accounts
req.statement: A customer shall transfer up to $25,000 per day between their own accounts; transfers above this shall be declined.
preconditions: source.owner == target.owner

doc.reasoning: The requirement defines a daily transfer limit of $25,000 for internal (same-owner) transfers. Key partitions are: (1) valid transfers within the limit, (2) transfers exceeding the daily limit (422), and (3) transfers on insufficient funds (409). Boundary values are $24,999.99 (below limit), $25,000.00 (at limit, valid), and $25,000.01 (above limit, invalid). State transitions cover account balance before and after a successful transfer to verify atomicity. A decision table covers the cross-product of limit-exceeded vs. insufficient-funds error paths. The violation fix changes test[5] (insufficient funds) from heuristic=STC to heuristic=DT, which correctly classifies this negative test as a decision-table-driven error pathway check. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that reset account state and daily totals before execution.

tests.name: test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Source account belongs to the same customer as target account
Source account balance >= $10,000.00
No prior transfers have been made today (daily total = $0)
expected: Response body contains transfer_id and status='completed'
trace: R-FIN-003: transfers up to $25,000 per day shall succeed
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_within_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, reset_accounts, reset_daily_limits):
    src_id, tgt_id = reset_accounts(owner="customer_1", src_balance=20000.00, tgt_balance=5000.00)
    reset_daily_limits(owner="customer_1")

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src_id,
        "target_account_id": tgt_id,
        "amount": 10000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert "transfer_id" in body
    assert body["status"] == "completed"

    src_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, src_id)
    tgt_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, tgt_id)
    assert src_balance == 10000.00
    assert tgt_balance == 15000.00

tests.name: test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
Source account balance >= $25,001.00
Cumulative transfers today total $0 (clean daily slate)
trace: R-FIN-003: transfers above $25,000 per day shall be declined
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_exceeding_daily_limit_returns_422(api_client, reset_accounts, reset_daily_limits):
    src_id, tgt_id = reset_accounts(owner="customer_2", src_balance=30000.00, tgt_balance=0.00)
    reset_daily_limits(owner="customer_2")

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src_id,
        "target_account_id": tgt_id,
        "amount": 25000.01,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"

    src_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, src_id)
    tgt_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, tgt_id)
    assert src_balance == 30000.00
    assert tgt_balance == 0.00

tests.name: test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
Source account balance >= $25,000.00
No prior transfers today (daily total = $0)
trace: R-FIN-003: up to $25,000 per day — boundary at exactly $25,000.00 must succeed
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_at_exact_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, reset_accounts, reset_daily_limits):
    src_id, tgt_id = reset_accounts(owner="customer_3", src_balance=25000.00, tgt_balance=0.00)
    reset_daily_limits(owner="customer_3")

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src_id,
        "target_account_id": tgt_id,
        "amount": 25000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "completed"

    src_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, src_id)
    tgt_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, tgt_id)
    assert src_balance == 0.00
    assert tgt_balance == 25000.00

tests.name: test_transfer_one_cent_below_daily_limit_succeeds
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
Source account balance >= $24,999.99
No prior transfers today (daily total = $0)
trace: R-FIN-003: $24,999.99 is one cent below the $25,000 boundary and must be accepted
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_one_cent_below_daily_limit_succeeds(api_client, reset_accounts, reset_daily_limits):
    src_id, tgt_id = reset_accounts(owner="customer_4", src_balance=25000.00, tgt_balance=0.00)
    reset_daily_limits(owner="customer_4")

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src_id,
        "target_account_id": tgt_id,
        "amount": 24999.99,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 201
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["status"] == "completed"

    src_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, src_id)
    tgt_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, tgt_id)
    assert abs(src_balance - 0.01) < 0.001
    assert abs(tgt_balance - 24999.99) < 0.001

tests.name: test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
Source account balance = $500.00 (below requested transfer amount)
No prior transfers today (daily total = $0, so limit is not the trigger)
trace: R-FIN-003: funds moved atomically — failed transfer must not partially debit
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_insufficient_funds_returns_409(api_client, reset_accounts, reset_daily_limits):
    src_id, tgt_id = reset_accounts(owner="customer_5", src_balance=500.00, tgt_balance=200.00)
    reset_daily_limits(owner="customer_5")

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src_id,
        "target_account_id": tgt_id,
        "amount": 1000.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 409
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "INSUFFICIENT_FUNDS"

    src_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, src_id)
    tgt_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, tgt_id)
    assert src_balance == 500.00
    assert tgt_balance == 200.00

tests.name: test_transfer_cumulative_daily_limit_exceeded_returns_422
preconditions: Customer is authenticated with valid JWT
Source and target accounts belong to the same customer
Source account balance >= $5,000.00
Customer has already transferred $24,000.00 today (cumulative daily total = $24,000.00)
actions: POST /transfers/internal with amount=1500.00, source_account_id, target_account_id (would bring daily total to $25,500.00)
trace: R-FIN-003: transfers above $25,000 per day shall be declined — cumulative limit pathway
tests.executable:
def test_transfer_cumulative_daily_limit_exceeded_returns_422(api_client, reset_accounts, reset_daily_limits, set_daily_total):
    src_id, tgt_id = reset_accounts(owner="customer_6", src_balance=10000.00, tgt_balance=0.00)
    reset_daily_limits(owner="customer_6")
    set_daily_total(owner="customer_6", total=24000.00)

    payload = {
        "source_account_id": src_id,
        "target_account_id": tgt_id,
        "amount": 1500.00,
        "currency": "USD"
    }
    resp = api_client.post("/transfers/internal", json=payload)

    assert resp.status_code == 422
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("error_code") == "DAILY_LIMIT_EXCEEDED"

    src_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, src_id)
    tgt_balance = get_account_balance(api_client, tgt_id)
    assert src_balance == 10000.00
    assert tgt_balance == 0.00
